I've uploaded a code repository in the directory /testbed. Consider the following PR description:

<pr_description>
[Feature]: zeroclaw props CLI for config property management
**Affected component:** runtime/daemon
**Severity:** S2 - degraded behavior

## Problem statement

There is no CLI command to manage individual config properties or set
encrypted secrets in config.toml. Users must either re-run the full
`zeroclaw onboard` wizard or manually edit config.toml.

## Solution (PR #5165)

Evolved from a secret-only CLI into a general-purpose config property
management system:

### Configurable derive macro (`crates/zeroclaw-macros/`)

- `#[derive(Configurable)]` with `#[secret]`, `#[nested]`, `#[prefix]` attributes
- Generates `prop_fields()`, `get_prop()`, `set_prop()`, `prop_is_secret()`,
  `init_defaults()`, `secret_fields()`, `encrypt_secrets()`, `decrypt_secrets()`
- `get_prop`/`set_prop` use serde roundtrip — macro generates only metadata,
  not per-field type dispatch
- `HasPropKind` trait maps types to `PropKind` at compile time; scalars in
  `traits.rs`, config enums via `impl_enum_prop_kind!`
- Handles `HashMap<String, T>` with `#[nested]` and `Vec<String>` with `#[secret]`
- All ~80 config structs annotated — every property individually addressable

### `zeroclaw props` CLI

- `props list [--filter PREFIX] [--secrets]` — enumerate all properties with values, types, categories
- `props get <path>` — get a value (secrets show set/unset status only)
- `props set <path> [value]` — set a property; secrets auto-detect masked input; enums offer interactive `dialoguer::Select`
- `props init [section]` — instantiate unconfigured sections with defaults (`enabled=false`)
- `props complete [partial]` — dynamic shell tab-completion for property paths

### Additional changes

- `enabled: bool` field on all channel configs for explicit activation
- `backfill_enabled()` auto-enables channels present in config.toml without explicit `enabled` key
- Shell completion hooks (bash/zsh/fish) wrap clap's generated function
- Manual encrypt/decrypt helpers replaced by macro-derived methods
- Exhaustive walk tests verify every prop and every enum variant

## Future work

- **`api_key_env` pattern replacement**: Several configs (Firecrawl, LinkedIn image providers, ImageGen) store env var names (`api_key_env: "STABILITY_API_KEY"`) instead of actual secret values. These should be replaced with `#[secret] api_key` fields that store the key directly (encrypted at rest), with env var fallback handled at runtime. This prevents users from accidentally putting actual keys in the env-var-name field.
- **`props unset`**: Dedicated subcommand to clear Option fields (currently works via `props set path ""` but not discoverable)
- **`props get` for secrets**: Decrypt and display secret values (with confirmation prompt)
- **Vec/HashMap field support in props**: Currently skipped — users can't set list or map fields via props
- **User-facing docs**: `docs/cli/props.md` reference page for the props subcommand

Part of umbrella tracker #4657.

## Non-goals

- Not a general key-value store
- Not a replacement for `zeroclaw onboard`

## Breaking change?

No — existing encrypted secrets continue to work. New `enabled` field
defaults to `false`; `backfill_enabled()` preserves existing channel
activation.

---

**Data hygiene checks:**
- [x] Removed personal/sensitive data from examples
- [x] Used neutral, project-focused wording

</pr_description>

  8942	impl Config {
  8943	    pub async fn load_or_init() -> Result<Self> {
  8944	        let (default_zeroclaw_dir, default_workspace_dir) = default_config_and_workspace_dirs()?;
  8945	
  8946	        let (zeroclaw_dir, workspace_dir, resolution_source) =
  8947	            resolve_runtime_config_dirs(&default_zeroclaw_dir, &default_workspace_dir).await?;
  8948	
  8949	        let config_path = zeroclaw_dir.join("config.toml");
  8950	
  8951	        fs::create_dir_all(&zeroclaw_dir)
  8952	            .await
  8953	            .with_context(|| config_dir_creation_error(&zeroclaw_dir))?;
  8954	        fs::create_dir_all(&workspace_dir)
  8955	            .await
  8956	            .context("Failed to create workspace directory")?;
  8957	
  8958	        ensure_bootstrap_files(&workspace_dir).await?;
  8959	
  8960	        if config_path.exists() {
  8961	            // Warn if config file is world-readable (may contain API keys)
  8962	            #[cfg(unix)]
  8963	            {
  8964	                use std::os::unix::fs::PermissionsExt;
  8965	                if let Ok(meta) = fs::metadata(&config_path).await {
  8966	                    if meta.permissions().mode() & 0o004 != 0 {
  8967	                        tracing::warn!(
  8968	                            "Config file {:?} is world-readable (mode {:o}). \
  8969	                             Consider restricting with: chmod 600 {:?}",
  8970	                            config_path,
  8971	                            meta.permissions().mode() & 0o777,
  8972	                            config_path,
  8973	                        );
  8974	                    }
  8975	                }
  8976	            }
  8977	
  8978	            let contents = fs::read_to_string(&config_path)
  8979	                .await
  8980	                .context("Failed to read config file")?;
  8981	
  8982	            // Deserialize the config with the standard TOML parser.
  8983	            //
  8984	            // Previously this used `serde_ignored::deserialize` for both
  8985	            // deserialization and unknown-key detection.  However,
  8986	            // `serde_ignored` silently drops field values inside nested
  8987	            // structs that carry `#[serde(default)]` (e.g. the entire
  8988	            // `[autonomy]` table), causing user-supplied values to be
  8989	            // replaced by defaults.  See #4171.
  8990	            //
  8991	            // We now deserialize with `toml::from_str` (which is correct)
  8992	            // and run `serde_ignored` separately just for diagnostics.
  8993	            let mut config: Config =
  8994	                toml::from_str(&contents).context("Failed to deserialize config file")?;
  8995	
  8996	            // Ensure the built-in default auto_approve entries are always
  8997	            // present.  When a user specifies `auto_approve` in their TOML
  8998	            // (e.g. to add a custom tool), serde replaces the default list
  8999	            // instead of merging.  This caused default-safe tools like
  9000	            // `weather` or `calculator` to lose their auto-approve status
  9001	            // and get silently denied in non-interactive channel runs.
  9002	            // See #4247.
  9003	            //
  9004	            // Users who want to require approval for a default tool can
  9005	            // add it to `always_ask`, which takes precedence over
  9006	            // `auto_approve` in the approval decision (see approval/mod.rs).
  9007	            config.autonomy.ensure_default_auto_approve();
  9008	
  9009	            // Detect unknown top-level config keys by comparing the raw
  9010	            // TOML table keys against what Config actually deserializes.
  9011	            // This replaces the previous serde_ignored-based approach which
  9012	            // had false-positive issues with #[serde(default)] nested structs.
  9013	            if let Ok(raw) = contents.parse::<toml::Table>() {
  9014	                // Build the set of known top-level keys from a default Config
  9015	                // serialization round-trip.  This is computed once and cached.
  9016	                static KNOWN_KEYS: OnceLock<Vec<String>> = OnceLock::new();
  9017	                let known = KNOWN_KEYS.get_or_init(|| {
  9018	                    toml::to_string(&Config::default())
  9019	                        .ok()
  9020	                        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<toml::Table>().ok())
  9021	                        .map(|t| t.keys().cloned().collect())
  9022	                        .unwrap_or_default()
  9023	                });
  9024	                for key in raw.keys() {
  9025	                    if !known.contains(key) {
  9026	                        tracing::warn!(
  9027	                            "Unknown config key ignored: \"{key}\". Check config.toml for typos or deprecated options.",
  9028	                        );
  9029	                    }
  9030	                }
  9031	            }
  9032	            // Set computed paths that are skipped during serialization
  9033	            config.config_path = config_path.clone();
  9034	            config.workspace_dir = workspace_dir;
  9035	            let store = crate::security::SecretStore::new(&zeroclaw_dir, config.secrets.encrypt);
  9036	            decrypt_optional_secret(&store, &mut config.api_key, "config.api_key")?;
  9037	            decrypt_optional_secret(
  9038	                &store,
  9039	                &mut config.composio.api_key,
  9040	                "config.composio.api_key",
  9041	            )?;
  9042	            if let Some(ref mut pinggy) = config.tunnel.pinggy {
  9043	                decrypt_optional_secret(&store, &mut pinggy.token, "config.tunnel.pinggy.token")?;
  9044	            }
  9045	            decrypt_optional_secret(
  9046	                &store,
  9047	                &mut config.microsoft365.client_secret,
  9048	                "config.microsoft365.client_secret",
  9049	            )?;
  9050	
  9051	            decrypt_optional_secret(
  9052	                &store,
  9053	                &mut config.browser.computer_use.api_key,
  9054	                "config.browser.computer_use.api_key",
  9055	            )?;
  9056	
  9057	            decrypt_optional_secret(
  9058	                &store,
  9059	                &mut config.web_search.brave_api_key,
  9060	                "config.web_search.brave_api_key",
  9061	            )?;
  9062

  9064	                &store,
  9065	                &mut config.storage.provider.config.db_url,

   245	    /// Browser automation configuration (`[browser]`).
   246	    #[serde(default)]
   247	    pub browser: BrowserConfig,
   248	
   249	    /// Browser delegation configuration (`[browser_delegate]`).
   250	    ///
   251	    /// Delegates browser-based tasks to a browser-capable CLI subprocess (e.g.
   252	    /// Claude Code with `claude-in-chrome` MCP tools). Useful for interacting
   253	    /// with corporate web apps (Teams, Outlook, Jira, Confluence) that lack
   254	    /// direct API access. A persistent Chrome profile can be configured so SSO
   255	    /// sessions survive across invocations.
   256	    ///
   257	    /// Fields:
   258	    /// - `enabled` (`bool`, default `false`) — enable the browser delegation tool.
   259	    /// - `cli_binary` (`String`, default `"claude"`) — CLI binary to spawn for browser tasks.
   260	    /// - `chrome_profile_dir` (`String`, default `""`) — Chrome user-data directory for
   261	    ///   persistent SSO sessions. When empty, a fresh profile is used each invocation.
   262	    /// - `allowed_domains` (`Vec<String>`, default `[]`) — allowlist of domains the browser
   263	    ///   may navigate to. Empty means all non-blocked domains are permitted.
   264	    /// - `blocked_domains` (`Vec<String>`, default `[]`) — denylist of domains. Blocked
   265	    ///   domains take precedence over allowed domains.
   266	    /// - `task_timeout_secs` (`u64`, default `120`) — per-task timeout in seconds.
   267	    ///
   268	    /// Compatibility: additive and disabled by default; existing configs remain valid when omitted.
   269	    /// Rollback/migration: remove `[browser_delegate]` or keep `enabled = false` to disable.

  6403	/// Telegram bot channel configuration.
  6404	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema)]
  6405	pub struct TelegramConfig {
  6406	    /// Telegram Bot API token (from @BotFather).
  6407	    pub bot_token: String,
  6408	    /// Allowed Telegram user IDs or usernames. Empty = deny all.
  6409	    pub allowed_users: Vec<String>,
  6410	    /// Streaming mode for progressive response delivery via message edits.
  6411	    #[serde(default)]
  6412	    pub stream_mode: StreamMode,
  6413	    /// Minimum interval (ms) between draft message edits to avoid rate limits.
  6414	    #[serde(default = "default_draft_update_interval_ms")]
  6415	    pub draft_update_interval_ms: u64,
  6416	    /// When true, a newer Telegram message from the same sender in the same chat
  6417	    /// cancels the in-flight request and starts a fresh response with preserved history.
  6418	    #[serde(default)]
  6419	    pub interrupt_on_new_message: bool,
  6420	    /// When true, only respond to messages that @-mention the bot in groups.
  6421	    /// Direct messages are always processed.
  6422	    #[serde(default)]
  6423	    pub mention_only: bool,
  6424	    /// Override for the top-level `ack_reactions` setting. When `None`, the
  6425	    /// channel falls back to `[channels_config].ack_reactions`. When set
  6426	    /// explicitly, it takes precedence.
  6427	    #[serde(default)]
  6428	    pub ack_reactions: Option<bool>,
  6429	    /// Per-channel proxy URL (http, https, socks5, socks5h).
  6430	    /// Overrides the global `[proxy]` setting for this channel only.
  6431	    #[serde(default)]
  6432	    pub proxy_url: Option<String>,
  6433	}
  6434	
  6435	impl ChannelConfig for TelegramConfig {
  6436	    fn name() -> &'static str {
  6437	        "Telegram"
  6438	    }
  6439	    fn desc() -> &'static str {
  6440	        "connect your bot"
  6441	    }
  6442	}
  6443	
  6444	/// Discord bot channel configuration.
  6445	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema)]
  6446	pub struct DiscordConfig {
  6447	    /// Discord bot token (from Discord Developer Portal).
  6448	    pub bot_token: String,
  6449	    /// Optional guild (server) ID to restrict the bot to a single guild.
  6450	    pub guild_id: Option<String>,
  6451	    /// Allowed Discord user IDs. Empty = deny all.
  6452	    #[serde(default)]
  6453	    pub allowed_users: Vec<String>,
  6454	    /// When true, process messages from other bots (not just humans).
  6455	    /// The bot still ignores its own messages to prevent feedback loops.
  6456	    #[serde(default)]
  6457	    pub listen_to_bots: bool,
  6458	    /// When true, a newer Discord message from the same sender in the same channel
  6459	    /// cancels the in-flight request and starts a fresh response with preserved history.
  6460	    #[serde(default)]
  6461	    pub interrupt_on_new_message: bool,
  6462	    /// When true, only respond to messages that @-mention the bot.
  6463	    /// Other messages in the guild are silently ignored.
  6464	    #[serde(default)]
  6465	    pub mention_only: bool,
  6466	    /// Per-channel proxy URL (http, https, socks5, socks5h).
  6467	    /// Overrides the global `[proxy]` setting for this channel only.
  6468	    #[serde(default)]
  6469	    pub proxy_url: Option<String>,
  6470	    /// Streaming mode for progressive response delivery.
  6471	    /// `off` (default): single message. `partial`: editable draft updates.
  6472	    /// `multi_message`: split response into separate messages at paragraph boundaries.
  6473	    #[serde(default)]
  6474	    pub stream_mode: StreamMode,
  6475	    /// Minimum interval (ms) between draft message edits to avoid rate limits.
  6476	    /// Only used when `stream_mode = "partial"`.
  6477	    #[serde(default = "default_draft_update_interval_ms")]
  6478	    pub draft_update_interval_ms: u64,
  6479	    /// Delay (ms) between sending each message chunk in multi-message mode.
  6480	    /// Only used when `stream_mode = "multi_message"`.
  6481	    #[serde(default = "default_multi_message_delay_ms")]
  6482	    pub multi_message_delay_ms: u64,
  6483	    /// Stall-watchdog timeout in seconds. When non-zero, the bot will abort
  6484	    /// and retry if no progress is made within this duration. 0 = disabled.
  6485	    #[serde(default)]

    /// Configure voice transcription.
    pub fn with_transcription(mut self, config: crate::config::TranscriptionConfig) -> Self {
        if !config.enabled {
            return self;
        }
        match super::transcription::TranscriptionManager::new(&config) {
            Ok(m) => {
                self.transcription_manager = Some(std::sync::Arc::new(m));
                self.transcription = Some(config);

    }

    /// Configure voice transcription for audio attachments.
    pub fn with_transcription(mut self, config: crate::config::TranscriptionConfig) -> Self {
        if !config.enabled {

  8752	fn decrypt_optional_secret(
  8753	    store: &crate::security::SecretStore,
  8754	    value: &mut Option<String>,
  8755	    field_name: &str,
  8756	) -> Result<()> {
  8757	    if let Some(raw) = value.clone() {
  8758	        if crate::security::SecretStore::is_encrypted(&raw) {
  8759	            *value = Some(
  8760	                store
  8761	                    .decrypt(&raw)
  8762	                    .with_context(|| format!("Failed to decrypt {field_name}"))?,
  8763	            );
  8764	        }
  8765	    }
  8766	    Ok(())
  8767	}

  6191	    pub session_backend: String,
  6192	    /// Auto-archive stale sessions older than this many hours. `0` disables. Default: `0`.
  6193	    #[serde(default)]
  6194	    pub session_ttl_hours: u32,
  6195	    /// Inbound message debounce window in milliseconds. When a sender fires
  6196	    /// multiple messages within this window, they are accumulated and dispatched
  6197	    /// as a single concatenated message. `0` disables debouncing. Default: `0`.
  6198	    #[serde(default)]
  6199	    pub debounce_ms: u64,
  6200	}

pub trait ChannelConfig {
    /// human-readable name
    fn name() -> &'"'"'static str;
    /// short description
    fn desc() -> &'"'"'static str;
}

// Maybe there should be a `&self` as parameter for custom channel/info or what...

pub trait ConfigHandle {
    fn name(&self) -> &'"'"'static str;
    fn desc(&self) -> &'"'"'static str;
}

/// User-facing primitive kind for a configurable property.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum PropKind {
    String,
    Integer,
    Float,
    Boolean,
    Array,
    Object,
    Null,
}

impl std::fmt::Display for PropKind {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'"'"'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
        let s = match self {
            Self::String => "string",
            Self::Integer => "integer",
            Self::Float => "float",
            Self::Boolean => "bool",
            Self::Array => "array",
            Self::Object => "object",
            Self::Null => "optional",
        };
        f.write_str(s)
    }
}

/// Metadata for a single property path in `config.toml`.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub struct PropField {
    pub path: String,
    pub kind: PropKind,
    pub secret: bool,
    pub category: String,
}

/// Runtime configurable-property access.
pub trait Configurable {
    fn prop_fields(&self) -> Vec<PropField>;
    fn get_prop(&self, path: &str) -> anyhow::Result<Option<serde_json::Value>>;
    fn set_prop(&mut self, path: &str, value: &str) -> anyhow::Result<()>;
    fn prop_is_secret(path: &str) -> bool;
    fn init_defaults(&mut self, section: Option<&str>) -> anyhow::Result<Vec<String>>;
    fn secret_fields(&self) -> Vec<PropField> {
        self.prop_fields().into_iter().filter(|f| f.secret).collect()
    }
    fn encrypt_secrets(&mut self) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
        Ok(())
    }
    fn decrypt_secrets(&mut self) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
        Ok(())
    }
}
'

fn props_secret_path_matches(pattern: &str, path: &str) -> bool {
    let pattern_parts: Vec<&str> = pattern.split('"'"'.'"'"').collect();
    let path_parts: Vec<&str> = path.split('"'"'.'"'"').collect();
    pattern_parts.len() == path_parts.len()
        && pattern_parts
            .iter()
            .zip(path_parts.iter())
            .all(|(pat, part)| *pat == "*" || pat == part)
}

fn props_is_secret_path(path: &str) -> bool {
    props_secret_paths()
        .iter()
        .any(|pattern| props_secret_path_matches(pattern, path))
}

fn prop_kind_from_value(value: &serde_json::Value) -> PropKind {
    match value {
        serde_json::Value::Null => PropKind::Null,
        serde_json::Value::Bool(_) => PropKind::Boolean,
        serde_json::Value::Number(n) if n.is_i64() || n.is_u64() => PropKind::Integer,
        serde_json::Value::Number(_) => PropKind::Float,
        serde_json::Value::String(_) => PropKind::String,
        serde_json::Value::Array(_) => PropKind::Array,
        serde_json::Value::Object(_) => PropKind::Object,
    }
}

fn walk_prop_fields(prefix: &str, value: &serde_json::Value, fields: &mut Vec<PropField>) {
    match value {
        serde_json::Value::Object(map) => {
            for (key, child) in map {
                let path = if prefix.is_empty() {
                    key.clone()
                } else {
                    format!("{prefix}.{key}")
                };
                walk_prop_fields(&path, child, fields);
            }
        }
        serde_json::Value::Array(_) => {
            // Lists are discoverable as properties, but individual elements are
            // intentionally not exposed as stable CLI property paths.
            fields.push(PropField {
                path: prefix.to_string(),
                kind: PropKind::Array,
                secret: props_is_secret_path(prefix),
                category: prefix.split('"'"'.'"'"').next().unwrap_or(prefix).to_string(),
            });
        }
        _ => fields.push(PropField {
            path: prefix.to_string(),
            kind: prop_kind_from_value(value),
            secret: props_is_secret_path(prefix),
            category: prefix.split('"'"'.'"'"').next().unwrap_or(prefix).to_string(),
        }),
    }
}

fn split_prop_path(path: &str) -> Result<Vec<&str>> {
    let parts: Vec<&str> = path.split('"'"'.'"'"').filter(|part| !part.is_empty()).collect();
    if parts.is_empty() || parts.join(".") != path {
        bail!("invalid property path: {path}");
    }
    Ok(parts)
}

fn json_get_path<'"'"'a>(value: &'"'"'a serde_json::Value, parts: &[&str]) -> Option<&'"'"'a serde_json::Value> {
    let mut current = value;
    for part in parts {
        current = current.as_object()?.get(*part)?;
    }
    Some(current)
}

fn coerce_prop_value(raw: &str, existing: Option<&serde_json::Value>) -> Result<serde_json::Value> {
    match existing {
        Some(serde_json::Value::Bool(_)) => raw
            .parse::<bool>()
            .map(serde_json::Value::Bool)
            .with_context(|| format!("expected boolean value, got {raw:?}")),
        Some(serde_json::Value::Number(n)) if n.is_i64() => raw
            .parse::<i64>()
            .map(serde_json::Number::from)
            .map(serde_json::Value::Number)
            .with_context(|| format!("expected integer value, got {raw:?}")),
        Some(serde_json::Value::Number(n)) if n.is_u64() => raw
            .parse::<u64>()
            .map(serde_json::Number::from)
            .map(serde_json::Value::Number)
            .with_context(|| format!("expected unsigned integer value, got {raw:?}")),
        Some(serde_json::Value::Number(_)) => raw
            .parse::<f64>()
            .ok()
            .and_then(serde_json::Number::from_f64)
            .map(serde_json::Value::Number)
            .with_context(|| format!("expected floating-point value, got {raw:?}")),
        Some(serde_json::Value::Array(_)) | Some(serde_json::Value::Object(_)) => {
            serde_json::from_str(raw).context("expected JSON array/object value")
        }
        Some(serde_json::Value::Null) => {
            if raw.is_empty() || raw.eq_ignore_ascii_case("null") {
                Ok(serde_json::Value::Null)
            } else if let Ok(v) = serde_json::from_str(raw) {
                Ok(v)
            } else {
                Ok(serde_json::Value::String(raw.to_string()))
            }
        }
        Some(serde_json::Value::String(_)) | None => Ok(serde_json::Value::String(raw.to_string())),
    }
}

fn json_set_path(
    value: &mut serde_json::Value,
    parts: &[&str],
    new_value: serde_json::Value,
) -> Result<()> {
    if parts.is_empty() {
        bail!("property path cannot be empty");
    }
    let mut current = value;
    for part in &parts[..parts.len() - 1] {
        current = current
            .as_object_mut()
            .and_then(|map| map.get_mut(*part))
            .with_context(|| format!("unknown property section: {part}"))?;
    }
    let key = parts[parts.len() - 1];
    let map = current
        .as_object_mut()
        .with_context(|| format!("property parent is not an object: {}", parts[..parts.len() - 1].join(".")))?;
    if !map.contains_key(key) {
        bail!("unknown property: {}", parts.join("."));
    }
    map.insert(key.to_string(), new_value);
    Ok(())
}

fn default_section_value(section: &str) -> Option<serde_json::Value> {
    let default = serde_json::to_value(Config::default()).ok()?;
    json_get_path(&default, &split_prop_path(section).ok()?).cloned()
}

  8859	        "channels_config.voice_call.auth_token",
  8860	    ]
  8861	}
  8862	
  8863	fn props_secret_path_matches(pattern: &str, path: &str) -> bool {
  8864	    let pattern_parts: Vec<&str> = pattern.split('.').collect();
  8865	    let path_parts: Vec<&str> = path.split('.').collect();
  8866	    pattern_parts.len() == path_parts.len()
  8867	        && pattern_parts
  8868	            .iter()
  8869	            .zip(path_parts.iter())
  8870	            .all(|(pat, part)| *pat == "*" || pat == part)
  8871	}
  8872	
  8873	fn props_is_secret_path(path: &str) -> bool {
  8874	    props_secret_paths()
  8875	        .iter()
  8876	        .any(|pattern| props_secret_path_matches(pattern, path))
  8877	}
  8878	
  8879	fn prop_kind_from_value(value: &serde_json::Value) -> PropKind {
  8880	    match value {
  8881	        serde_json::Value::Null => PropKind::Null,
  8882	        serde_json::Value::Bool(_) => PropKind::Boolean,
  8883	        serde_json::Value::Number(n) if n.is_i64() || n.is_u64() => PropKind::Integer,
  8884	        serde_json::Value::Number(_) => PropKind::Float,
  8885	        serde_json::Value::String(_) => PropKind::String,
  8886	        serde_json::Value::Array(_) => PropKind::Array,
  8887	        serde_json::Value::Object(_) => PropKind::Object,
  8888	    }
  8889	}
  8890	
  8891	fn walk_prop_fields(prefix: &str, value: &serde_json::Value, fields: &mut Vec<PropField>) {
  8892	    match value {
  8893	        serde_json::Value::Object(map) => {
  8894	            for (key, child) in map {
  8895	                let path = if prefix.is_empty() {
  8896	                    key.clone()
  8897	                } else {
  8898	                    format!("{prefix}.{key}")
  8899	                };
  8900	                walk_prop_fields(&path, child, fields);
  8901	            }
  8902	        }
  8903	        serde_json::Value::Array(_) => {
  8904	            // Lists are discoverable as properties, but individual elements are
  8905	            // intentionally not exposed as stable CLI property paths.
  8906	            fields.push(PropField {
  8907	                path: prefix.to_string(),
  8908	                kind: PropKind::Array,
  8909	                secret: props_is_secret_path(prefix),
  8910	                category: prefix.split('.').next().unwrap_or(prefix).to_string(),
  8911	            });
  8912	        }
  8913	        _ => fields.push(PropField {
  8914	            path: prefix.to_string(),
  8915	            kind: prop_kind_from_value(value),
  8916	            secret: props_is_secret_path(prefix),
  8917	            category: prefix.split('.').next().unwrap_or(prefix).to_string(),
  8918	        }),
  8919	    }
  8920	}
  8921	
  8922	fn split_prop_path(path: &str) -> Result<Vec<&str>> {
  8923	    let parts: Vec<&str> = path.split('.').filter(|part| !part.is_empty()).collect();
  8924	    if parts.is_empty() || parts.join(".") != path {
  8925	        bail!("invalid property path: {path}");
  8926	    }
  8927	    Ok(parts)
  8928	}
  8929	
  8930	fn json_get_path<'a>(value: &'a serde_json::Value, parts: &[&str]) -> Option<&'a serde_json::Value> {
  8931	    let mut current = value;
  8932	    for part in parts {
  8933	        current = current.as_object()?.get(*part)?;
  8934	    }
  8935	    Some(current)
  8936	}
  8937	
  8938	fn coerce_prop_value(raw: &str, existing: Option<&serde_json::Value>) -> Result<serde_json::Value> {
  8939	    match existing {
  8940	        Some(serde_json::Value::Bool(_)) => raw
  8941	            .parse::<bool>()
  8942	            .map(serde_json::Value::Bool)
  8943	            .with_context(|| format!("expected boolean value, got {raw:?}")),
  8944	        Some(serde_json::Value::Number(n)) if n.is_i64() => raw
  8945	            .parse::<i64>()
  8946	            .map(serde_json::Number::from)
  8947	            .map(serde_json::Value::Number)
  8948	            .with_context(|| format!("expected integer value, got {raw:?}")),
  8949	        Some(serde_json::Value::Number(n)) if n.is_u64() => raw
  8950	            .parse::<u64>()
  8951	            .map(serde_json::Number::from)
  8952	            .map(serde_json::Value::Number)
  8953	            .with_context(|| format!("expected unsigned integer value, got {raw:?}")),
  8954	        Some(serde_json::Value::Number(_)) => raw
  8955	            .parse::<f64>()
  8956	            .ok()
  8957	            .and_then(serde_json::Number::from_f64)
  8958	            .map(serde_json::Value::Number)
  8959	            .with_context(|| format!("expected floating-point value, got {raw:?}")),
  8960	        Some(serde_json::Value::Array(_)) | Some(serde_json::Value::Object(_)) => {
  8961	            serde_json::from_str(raw).context("expected JSON array/object value")
  8962	        }
  8963	        Some(serde_json::Value::Null) => {
  8964	            if raw.is_empty() || raw.eq_ignore_ascii_case("null") {
  8965	                Ok(serde_json::Value::Null)
  8966	            } else if let Ok(v) = serde_json::from_str(raw) {
  8967	                Ok(v)
  8968	            } else {
  8969	                Ok(serde_json::Value::String(raw.to_string()))
  8970	            }
  8971	        }
  8972	        Some(serde_json::Value::String(_)) | None => Ok(serde_json::Value::String(raw.to_string())),
  8973	    }
  8974	}
  8975	
  8976	fn json_set_path(
  8977	    value: &mut serde_json::Value,
  8978	    parts: &[&str],
  8979	    new_value: serde_json::Value,
  8980	) -> Result<()> {
  8981	    if parts.is_empty() {
  8982	        bail!("property path cannot be empty");
  8983	    }
  8984	    let mut current = value;
  8985	    for part in &parts[..parts.len() - 1] {
  8986	        current = current
  8987	            .as_object_mut()
  8988	            .and_then(|map| map.get_mut(*part))
  8989	            .with_context(|| format!("unknown property section: {part}"))?;
  8990	    }
  8991	    let key = parts[parts.len() - 1];
  8992	    let map = current
  8993	        .as_object_mut()
  8994	        .with_context(|| format!("property parent is not an object: {}", parts[..parts.len() - 1].join(".")))?;
  8995	    if !map.contains_key(key) {
  8996	        bail!("unknown property: {}", parts.join("."));
  8997	    }
  8998	    map.insert(key.to_string(), new_value);
  8999	    Ok(())
  9000	}
  9001	
  9002	fn default_section_value(section: &str) -> Option<serde_json::Value> {
  9003	    let default = serde_json::to_value(Config::default()).ok()?;
  9004	    json_get_path(&default, &split_prop_path(section).ok()?).cloned()
  9005	}
  9006

impl Configurable for Config {
    fn prop_fields(&self) -> Vec<PropField> {
        let Ok(value) = serde_json::to_value(self) else {
            return Vec::new();
        };
        let mut fields = Vec::new();
        walk_prop_fields("", &value, &mut fields);
        fields.sort_by(|a, b| a.path.cmp(&b.path));
        fields
    }

    fn get_prop(&self, path: &str) -> Result<Option<serde_json::Value>> {
        let parts = split_prop_path(path)?;
        let value = serde_json::to_value(self).context("failed to serialize config")?;
        Ok(json_get_path(&value, &parts).cloned())
    }

    fn set_prop(&mut self, path: &str, raw_value: &str) -> Result<()> {
        let parts = split_prop_path(path)?;
        if matches!(parts.first(), Some(&"workspace_dir" | &"config_path")) {
            bail!("{path} is computed at runtime and cannot be set");
        }

        let mut value = serde_json::to_value(&*self).context("failed to serialize config")?;
        let existing = json_get_path(&value, &parts).cloned();
        if existing.is_none() {
            bail!("unknown property: {path}");
        }
        let new_value = coerce_prop_value(raw_value, existing.as_ref())?;
        json_set_path(&mut value, &parts, new_value)?;

        let mut updated: Config = serde_json::from_value(value).context("failed to update config")?;
        updated.workspace_dir = self.workspace_dir.clone();
        updated.config_path = self.config_path.clone();
        *self = updated;
        Ok(())
    }

    fn prop_is_secret(path: &str) -> bool {
        props_is_secret_path(path)
    }

    fn init_defaults(&mut self, section: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<String>> {
        let mut initialized = Vec::new();
        let mut value = serde_json::to_value(&*self).context("failed to serialize config")?;

        if let Some(section) = section {
            let parts = split_prop_path(section)?;
            let current = json_get_path(&value, &parts)
                .with_context(|| format!("unknown config section: {section}"))?;
            if !current.is_null() {
                return Ok(initialized);
            }
            let default_value = default_section_value(section)
                .with_context(|| format!("no default is available for section {section}"))?;
            json_set_path(&mut value, &parts, default_value)?;
            initialized.push(section.to_string());
        } else {
            let default = serde_json::to_value(Config::default()).context("failed to serialize defaults")?;
            if let (Some(channels), Some(default_channels)) = (
                value
                    .get_mut("channels_config")
                    .and_then(serde_json::Value::as_object_mut),
                default
                    .get("channels_config")
                    .and_then(serde_json::Value::as_object),
            ) {
                for (name, current) in channels.iter_mut() {
                    if current.is_null() {
                        if let Some(default_value) = default_channels.get(name) {
                            if !default_value.is_null() {
                                *current = default_value.clone();
                                initialized.push(format!("channels_config.{name}"));
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        if !initialized.is_empty() {
            let mut updated: Config = serde_json::from_value(value).context("failed to initialize config defaults")?;
            updated.workspace_dir = self.workspace_dir.clone();
            updated.config_path = self.config_path.clone();
            *self = updated;
        }
        Ok(initialized)
    }
}

 11102	impl Configurable for Config {
 11103	    fn prop_fields(&self) -> Vec<PropField> {
 11104	        let Ok(value) = serde_json::to_value(self) else {
 11105	            return Vec::new();
 11106	        };
 11107	        let mut fields = Vec::new();
 11108	        walk_prop_fields("", &value, &mut fields);
 11109	        fields.sort_by(|a, b| a.path.cmp(&b.path));
 11110	        fields
 11111	    }
 11112	
 11113	    fn get_prop(&self, path: &str) -> Result<Option<serde_json::Value>> {
 11114	        let parts = split_prop_path(path)?;
 11115	        let value = serde_json::to_value(self).context("failed to serialize config")?;
 11116	        Ok(json_get_path(&value, &parts).cloned())
 11117	    }
 11118	
 11119	    fn set_prop(&mut self, path: &str, raw_value: &str) -> Result<()> {
 11120	        let parts = split_prop_path(path)?;
 11121	        if matches!(parts.first(), Some(&"workspace_dir" | &"config_path")) {
 11122	            bail!("{path} is computed at runtime and cannot be set");
 11123	        }
 11124	
 11125	        let mut value = serde_json::to_value(&*self).context("failed to serialize config")?;
 11126	        let existing = json_get_path(&value, &parts).cloned();
 11127	        if existing.is_none() {
 11128	            bail!("unknown property: {path}");
 11129	        }
 11130	        let new_value = coerce_prop_value(raw_value, existing.as_ref())?;
 11131	        json_set_path(&mut value, &parts, new_value)?;
 11132	
 11133	        let mut updated: Config = serde_json::from_value(value).context("failed to update config")?;
 11134	        updated.workspace_dir = self.workspace_dir.clone();
 11135	        updated.config_path = self.config_path.clone();
 11136	        *self = updated;
 11137	        Ok(())
 11138	    }
 11139	
 11140	    fn prop_is_secret(path: &str) -> bool {
 11141	        props_is_secret_path(path)
 11142	    }
 11143	
 11144	    fn init_defaults(&mut self, section: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<String>> {
 11145	        let mut initialized = Vec::new();
 11146	        let mut value = serde_json::to_value(&*self).context("failed to serialize config")?;
 11147	
 11148	        if let Some(section) = section {
 11149	            let parts = split_prop_path(section)?;
 11150	            let current = json_get_path(&value, &parts)
 11151	                .with_context(|| format!("unknown config section: {section}"))?;
 11152	            if !current.is_null() {
 11153	                return Ok(initialized);
 11154	            }
 11155	            let default_value = default_section_value(section)
 11156	                .with_context(|| format!("no default is available for section {section}"))?;
 11157	            json_set_path(&mut value, &parts, default_value)?;
 11158	            initialized.push(section.to_string());
 11159	        } else {
 11160	            let default = serde_json::to_value(Config::default()).context("failed to serialize defaults")?;
 11161	            if let (Some(channels), Some(default_channels)) = (
 11162	                value
 11163	                    .get_mut("channels_config")
 11164	                    .and_then(serde_json::Value::as_object_mut),
 11165	                default
 11166	                    .get("channels_config")
 11167	                    .and_then(serde_json::Value::as_object),
 11168	            ) {
 11169	                for (name, current) in channels.iter_mut() {
 11170	                    if current.is_null() {
 11171	                        if let Some(default_value) = default_channels.get(name) {
 11172	                            if !default_value.is_null() {
 11173	                                *current = default_value.clone();
 11174	                                initialized.push(format!("channels_config.{name}"));
 11175	                            }
 11176	                        }
 11177	                    }
 11178	                }
 11179	            }
 11180	        }
 11181	
 11182	        if !initialized.is_empty() {
 11183	            let mut updated: Config = serde_json::from_value(value).context("failed to initialize config defaults")?;
 11184	            updated.workspace_dir = self.workspace_dir.clone();
 11185	            updated.config_path = self.config_path.clone();
 11186	            *self = updated;
 11187	        }
 11188	        Ok(initialized)
 11189	    }
 11190	}

 11234	#[allow(clippy::unused_async)] // async needed on unix for tokio File I/O; no-op on other platforms
 11235	async fn sync_directory(path: &Path) -> Result<()> {
 11236	    #[cfg(unix)]
 11237	    {
 11238	        let dir = File::open(path)
 11239	            .await
 11240	            .with_context(|| format!("Failed to open directory for fsync: {}", path.display()))?;
 11241	        dir.sync_all()
 11242	            .await
 11243	            .with_context(|| format!("Failed to fsync directory metadata: {}", path.display()))?;
 11244	        Ok(())
 11245	    }
 11246	
 11247	    #[cfg(windows)]
 11248	    {
 11249	        use std::os::windows::fs::OpenOptionsExt;
 11250	        const FILE_FLAG_BACKUP_SEMANTICS: u32 = 0x02000000;
 11251	        let dir = std::fs::OpenOptions::new()
 11252	            .read(true)
 11253	            .custom_flags(FILE_FLAG_BACKUP_SEMANTICS)
 11254	            .open(path)
 11255	            .with_context(|| format!("Failed to open directory for fsync: {}", path.display()))?;
 11256	        // FlushFileBuffers on directory handles returns ERROR_ACCESS_DENIED on
 11257	        // Windows (OS Error 5). This is expected — NTFS does not support
 11258	        // flushing directory metadata the same way Unix does. The individual
 11259	        // files have already been synced, so it is safe to ignore this error.
 11260	        if let Err(e) = dir.sync_all() {
 11261	            if e.raw_os_error() == Some(5) {
 11262	                tracing::trace!(
 11263	                    "Ignoring expected ACCESS_DENIED when fsyncing directory on Windows: {}",
 11264	                    path.display()
 11265	                );
 11266	            } else {
 11267	                return Err(e).with_context(|| {
 11268	                    format!("Failed to fsync directory metadata: {}", path.display())
 11269	                });
 11270	            }

#[allow(clippy::unused_async)] // async needed on unix for tokio File I/O; no-op on other platforms
async fn sync_directory(path: &Path) -> Result<()> {
    #[cfg(unix)]
    {
        let dir = File::open(path)
            .await
            .with_context(|| format!("Failed to open directory for fsync: {}", path.display()))?;
        dir.sync_all()
            .await
            .with_context(|| format!("Failed to fsync directory metadata: {}", path.display()))?;
        Ok(())
    }

    #[cfg(windows)]
    {
        use std::os::windows::fs::OpenOptionsExt;
        const FILE_FLAG_BACKUP_SEMANTICS: u32 = 0x02000000;
        let dir = std::fs::OpenOptions::new()
            .read(true)
            .custom_flags(FILE_FLAG_BACKUP_SEMANTICS)
            .open(path)
            .with_context(|| format!("Failed to open directory for fsync: {}", path.display()))?;
        // FlushFileBuffers on directory handles returns ERROR_ACCESS_DENIED on
        // Windows (OS Error 5). This is expected — NTFS does not support
        // flushing directory metadata the same way Unix does. The individual
        // files have already been synced, so it is safe to ignore this error.
        if let Err(e) = dir.sync_all() {
            if e.raw_os_error() == Some(5) {
                tracing::trace!(
                    "Ignoring expected ACCESS_DENIED when fsyncing directory on Windows: {}",
                    path.display()
                );
            } else {
                return Err(e).with_context(|| {
                    format!("Failed to fsync directory metadata: {}", path.display())
                });
            }
        }
        Ok(())
    }

    #[cfg(not(any(unix, windows)))]
    {
        let _ = path;
        Ok(())
    }
}

// ── SOP engine configuration ───────────────────────────────────

/// Standard Operating Procedures engine configuration (`[sop]`).
///
/// The `default_execution_mode` field uses the `SopExecutionMode` type from
/// `sop::types` (re-exported via `sop::SopExecutionMode`). To avoid circular
/// module references, config stores it using the same enum definition.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema)]
pub struct SopConfig {
    /// Directory containing SOP definitions (subdirs with SOP.toml + SOP.md).
    /// Falls back to `<workspace>/sops` when omitted.
    #[serde(default)]
    pub sops_dir: Option<String>,

    /// Default execution mode for SOPs that omit `execution_mode`.
    /// Values: `auto`, `supervised` (default), `step_by_step`,
    /// `priority_based`, `deterministic`.
    #[serde(default = "default_sop_execution_mode")]
    pub default_execution_mode: String,

    /// Maximum total concurrent SOP runs across all SOPs.
    #[serde(default = "default_sop_max_concurrent_total")]
    pub max_concurrent_total: usize,

    /// Approval timeout in seconds. When a run waits for approval longer than
    /// this, Critical/High-priority SOPs auto-approve; others stay waiting.
    /// Set to 0 to disable timeout.
    #[serde(default = "default_sop_approval_timeout_secs")]
    pub approval_timeout_secs: u64,

    /// Maximum number of finished runs kept in memory for status queries.
    /// Oldest runs are evicted when over capacity. 0 = unlimited.
    #[serde(default = "default_sop_max_finished_runs")]
    pub max_finished_runs: usize,
}

fn default_sop_execution_mode() -> String {
    "supervised".to_string()
}

fn default_sop_max_concurrent_total() -> usize {
    4
}

            if let (Some(channels), Some(default_channels)) = (
                value
                    .get_mut("channels_config")
                    .and_then(serde_json::Value::as_object_mut),

                    if current.is_null() {
                        if let Some(default_value) = default_channels.get(name) {
                            if !default_value.is_null() {
                                *current = default_value.clone();
                                initialized.push(format!("channels_config.{name}"));
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        if !initialized.is_empty() {
            let mut updated: Config = serde_json::from_value(value).context("failed to initialize config defaults")?;
            updated.workspace_dir = self.workspace_dir.clone();
            updated.config_path = self.config_path.clone();
            *self = updated;
        }
        Ok(initialized)
    }
}

str_replace_editor insert /testbed/src/main.rs    --new_str '
async fn handle_props_command(props_command: PropsCommands, config: &mut Config) -> Result<()> {
    match props_command {
        PropsCommands::List { filter, secrets } => {
            for field in config.prop_fields() {
                if !secrets && field.secret {
                    continue;
                }
                if let Some(prefix) = filter.as_deref() {
                    if !field.path.starts_with(prefix) {
                        continue;
                    }
                }
                let value = if field.secret {
                    match config.get_prop(&field.path)? {
                        Some(serde_json::Value::Null) | None => "<unset>".to_string(),
                        Some(serde_json::Value::String(s)) if s.is_empty() => "<unset>".to_string(),
                        Some(_) => "<set>".to_string(),
                    }
                } else {
                    config
                        .get_prop(&field.path)?
                        .map(|v| prop_value_to_display(&v))
                        .unwrap_or_else(|| "<unset>".to_string())
                };
                println!("{}\t{}\t{}\t{}", field.path, field.kind, field.category, value);
            }
            Ok(())
        }
        PropsCommands::Get { path } => {
            let value = config
                .get_prop(&path)?
                .with_context(|| format!("unknown property: {path}"))?;
            if Config::prop_is_secret(&path) {
                let status = match value {
                    serde_json::Value::Null => "unset",
                    serde_json::Value::String(ref s) if s.is_empty() => "unset",
                    _ => "set",
                };
                println!("{status}");
            } else {
                println!("{}", prop_value_to_display(&value));
            }
            Ok(())
        }
        PropsCommands::Set { path, value } => {
            let value = match value {
                Some(v) => v,
                None if Config::prop_is_secret(&path) => Password::new()
                    .with_prompt(format!("Value for {path}"))
                    .interact()
                    .context("failed to read secret value")?,
                None => bail!("missing value for non-secret property {path}"),
            };
            config.set_prop(&path, &value)?;
            config.save().await?;
            println!("Updated {path}");
            Ok(())
        }
        PropsCommands::Init { section } => {
            let initialized = config.init_defaults(section.as_deref())?;
            if initialized.is_empty() {
                println!("Nothing to initialize");
            } else {
                config.save().await?;
                for path in initialized {
                    println!("Initialized {path}");
                }
            }
            Ok(())
        }
        PropsCommands::Complete { partial } => {
            let partial = partial.unwrap_or_default();
            for field in config.prop_fields() {
                if field.path.starts_with(&partial) {
                    println!("{}", field.path);
                }
            }
            Ok(())
        }
    }
}

fn prop_value_to_display(value: &serde_json::Value) -> String {
    match value {
        serde_json::Value::String(s) => s.clone(),
        serde_json::Value::Null => "<unset>".to_string(),
        _ => serde_json::to_string(value).unwrap_or_else(|_| value.to_string()),
    }
}

The file /testbed/src/main.rs has been edited. Here's the result of running `cat -n` on a snippet of the edited file:

  1668	        },

  1674	        PropsCommands::List { filter, secrets } => {
  1675	            for field in config.prop_fields() {
  1676	                if !secrets && field.secret {
  1677	                    continue;
  1678	                }
  1679	                if let Some(prefix) = filter.as_deref() {
  1680	                    if !field.path.starts_with(prefix) {
  1681	                        continue;
  1682	                    }
  1683	                }
  1684	                let value = if field.secret {
  1685	                    match config.get_prop(&field.path)? {
  1686	                        Some(serde_json::Value::Null) | None => "<unset>".to_string(),
  1687	                        Some(serde_json::Value::String(s)) if s.is_empty() => "<unset>".to_string(),
  1688	                        Some(_) => "<set>".to_string(),
  1689	                    }
  1690	                } else {
  1691	                    config
  1692	                        .get_prop(&field.path)?
  1693	                        .map(|v| prop_value_to_display(&v))
  1694	                        .unwrap_or_else(|| "<unset>".to_string())
  1695	                };
  1696	                println!("{}\t{}\t{}\t{}", field.path, field.kind, field.category, value);
  1697	            }
  1698	            Ok(())
  1699	        }
  1700	        PropsCommands::Get { path } => {
  1701	            let value = config
  1702	                .get_prop(&path)?
  1703	                .with_context(|| format!("unknown property: {path}"))?;
  1704	            if Config::prop_is_secret(&path) {
  1705	                let status = match value {
  1706	                    serde_json::Value::Null => "unset",
  1707	                    serde_json::Value::String(ref s) if s.is_empty() => "unset",
  1708	                    _ => "set",
  1709	                };
  1710	                println!("{status}");
  1711	            } else {
  1712	                println!("{}", prop_value_to_display(&value));
  1713	            }
  1714	            Ok(())
  1715	        }
  1716	        PropsCommands::Set { path, value } => {
  1717	            let value = match value {
  1718	                Some(v) => v,
  1719	                None if Config::prop_is_secret(&path) => Password::new()
  1720	                    .with_prompt(format!("Value for {path}"))
  1721	                    .interact()
  1722	                    .context("failed to read secret value")?,
  1723	                None => bail!("missing value for non-secret property {path}"),
  1724	            };
  1725	            config.set_prop(&path, &value)?;
  1726	            config.save().await?;
  1727	            println!("Updated {path}");
  1728	            Ok(())
  1729	        }
  1730	        PropsCommands::Init { section } => {
  1731	            let initialized = config.init_defaults(section.as_deref())?;
  1732	            if initialized.is_empty() {
  1733	                println!("Nothing to initialize");
  1734	            } else {
  1735	                config.save().await?;
  1736	                for path in initialized {
  1737	                    println!("Initialized {path}");
  1738	                }
  1739	            }
  1740	            Ok(())
  1741	        }
  1742	        PropsCommands::Complete { partial } => {
  1743	            let partial = partial.unwrap_or_default();
  1744	            for field in config.prop_fields() {
  1745	                if field.path.starts_with(&partial) {
  1746	                    println!("{}", field.path);
  1747	                }
  1748	            }
  1749	            Ok(())
  1750	        }
  1751	    }
  1752	}
  1753	
  1754	fn prop_value_to_display(value: &serde_json::Value) -> String {
  1755	    match value {
  1756	        serde_json::Value::String(s) => s.clone(),
  1757	        serde_json::Value::Null => "<unset>".to_string(),
  1758	        _ => serde_json::to_string(value).unwrap_or_else(|_| value.to_string()),
  1759	    }
  1760	}
  1761	
  1762	        Commands::Plugin { plugin_command } => match plugin_command {
  1763	            PluginCommands::List => {
  1764	                let host = zeroclaw::plugins::host::PluginHost::new(&config.workspace_dir)?;
  1765	                let plugins = host.list_plugins();

fn prop_value_to_display(value: &serde_json::Value) -> String {
    match value {
        serde_json::Value::String(s) => s.clone(),
        serde_json::Value::Null => "<unset>".to_string(),
        _ => serde_json::to_string(value).unwrap_or_else(|_| value.to_string()),
    }
}

#[tokio::main]
#[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)]
async fn main() -> Result<()> {
    // Install default crypto provider for Rustls TLS.
    // This prevents the error: "could not automatically determine the process-level CryptoProvider"
    // when both aws-lc-rs and ring features are available (or neither is explicitly selected).
    if let Err(e) = rustls::crypto::ring::default_provider().install_default() {
        eprintln!("Warning: Failed to install default crypto provider: {e:?}");
    }

    if std::env::args_os().len() <= 1 {
        return print_no_command_help();
    }

    let cli = Cli::parse();

    if let Some(config_dir) = &cli.config_dir {
        if config_dir.trim().is_empty() {
            bail!("--config-dir cannot be empty");
        }
        // SAFETY: called early in main before any threads are spawned.
        unsafe { std::env::set_var("ZEROCLAW_CONFIG_DIR", config_dir) };
    }

    // Completions must remain stdout-only and should not load config or initialize logging.
    // This avoids warnings/log lines corrupting sourced completion scripts.
    if let Commands::Completions { shell } = &cli.command {
        let mut stdout = std::io::stdout().lock();
        write_shell_completion(*shell, &mut stdout)?;
        return Ok(());
    }

    // Initialize logging - respects RUST_LOG env var, defaults to INFO
    let subscriber = fmt::Subscriber::builder()
        .with_env_filter(
            EnvFilter::try_from_default_env().unwrap_or_else(|_| EnvFilter::new("info")),
        )
        .finish();

    tracing::subscriber::set_global_default(subscriber).expect("setting default subscriber failed");

    // Onboard auto-detects the environment: if stdin/stdout are a TTY and no
    // provider flags were given, it runs the full interactive wizard; otherwise
    // it runs the quick (scriptable) setup.  Use --quick to force quick setup,
    // or set ZEROCLAW_INTERACTIVE=1 to force interactive mode when TTY
    // detection fails.  This means `curl … | bash` and
    // `zeroclaw onboard --api-key …` both take the fast path, while a bare
    // `zeroclaw onboard` in a terminal launches the wizard.
    if let Commands::Onboard {
        force,
        reinit,
        channels_only,
        api_key,

+fn props_secret_path_matches(pattern: &str, path: &str) -> bool {
+    let pattern_parts: Vec<&str> = pattern.split('.').collect();
+    let path_parts: Vec<&str> = path.split('.').collect();
+    pattern_parts.len() == path_parts.len()
+        && pattern_parts
+            .iter()
+            .zip(path_parts.iter())
+            .all(|(pat, part)| *pat == "*" || pat == part)
+}
+
+fn props_is_secret_path(path: &str) -> bool {
+    props_secret_paths()
+        .iter()
+        .any(|pattern| props_secret_path_matches(pattern, path))
+}
+
+fn props_channel_names() -> &'static [&'static str] {
+    &[
+        "telegram",
+        "discord",
+        "discord_history",
+        "slack",
+        "mattermost",
+        "webhook",
+        "imessage",
+        "matrix",
+        "signal",
+        "whatsapp",
+        "linq",
+        "wati",
+        "nextcloud_talk",
+        "email",
+        "gmail_push",
+        "irc",
+        "lark",
+        "feishu",
+        "dingtalk",
+        "wecom",
+        "qq",
+        "twitter",
+        "mochat",
+        "clawdtalk",
+        "reddit",
+        "bluesky",
+        "voice_call",
+        "mqtt",
+    ]
+}
+
+fn is_virtual_channel_enabled_path(parts: &[&str]) -> bool {
+    parts.len() == 3
+        && parts[0] == "channels_config"
+        && parts[2] == "enabled"
+        && props_channel_names().contains(&parts[1])
+}
+
+
+fn prop_kind_from_value(value: &serde_json::Value) -> PropKind {
+    match value {
+        serde_json::Value::Null => PropKind::Null,
+        serde_json::Value::Bool(_) => PropKind::Boolean,
+        serde_json::Value::Number(n) if n.is_i64() || n.is_u64() => PropKind::Integer,
+        serde_json::Value::Number(_) => PropKind::Float,
+        serde_json::Value::String(_) => PropKind::String,
+        serde_json::Value::Array(_) => PropKind::Array,
+        serde_json::Value::Object(_) => PropKind::Object,
+    }
+}
+
+fn walk_prop_fields(prefix: &str, value: &serde_json::Value, fields: &mut Vec<PropField>) {
+    match value {
+        serde_json::Value::Object(map) => {
+            if prefix == "channels_config" {
+                for channel in props_channel_names() {
+                    fields.push(PropField {
+                        path: format!("channels_config.{channel}.enabled"),
+                        kind: PropKind::Boolean,
+                        secret: false,
+                        category: "channels_config".to_string(),
+                    });
+                }
+            }
+            for (key, child) in map {
+                let path = if prefix.is_empty() {
+                    key.clone()
+                } else {
+                    format!("{prefix}.{key}")
+                };
+                walk_prop_fields(&path, child, fields);
+            }
+        }
+        serde_json::Value::Array(_) => {
+            // Lists are discoverable as properties, but individual elements are
+            // intentionally not exposed as stable CLI property paths.
+            fields.push(PropField {
+                path: prefix.to_string(),
+                kind: PropKind::Array,
+                secret: props_is_secret_path(prefix),
+                category: prefix.split('.').next().unwrap_or(prefix).to_string(),
+            });
+        }
+        _ => fields.push(PropField {
+            path: prefix.to_string(),
+            kind: prop_kind_from_value(value),
+            secret: props_is_secret_path(prefix),
+            category: prefix.split('.').next().unwrap_or(prefix).to_string(),
+        }),
+    }
+}
+
+fn split_prop_path(path: &str) -> Result<Vec<&str>> {
+    let parts: Vec<&str> = path.split('.').filter(|part| !part.is_empty()).collect();
+    if parts.is_empty() || parts.join(".") != path {
+        bail!("invalid property path: {path}");
+    }
+    Ok(parts)
+}
+
+fn json_get_path<'a>(value: &'a serde_json::Value, parts: &[&str]) -> Option<&'a serde_json::Value> {
+    let mut current = value;
+    for part in parts {
+        current = current.as_object()?.get(*part)?;
+    }
+    Some(current)
+}
+
+fn coerce_prop_value(raw: &str, existing: Option<&serde_json::Value>) -> Result<serde_json::Value> {
+    match existing {
+        Some(serde_json::Value::Bool(_)) => raw
+            .parse::<bool>()
+            .map(serde_json::Value::Bool)
+            .with_context(|| format!("expected boolean value, got {raw:?}")),
+        Some(serde_json::Value::Number(n)) if n.is_i64() => raw
+            .parse::<i64>()
+            .map(serde_json::Number::from)
+            .map(serde_json::Value::Number)
+            .with_context(|| format!("expected integer value, got {raw:?}")),
+        Some(serde_json::Value::Number(n)) if n.is_u64() => raw
+            .parse::<u64>()
+            .map(serde_json::Number::from)
+            .map(serde_json::Value::Number)
+            .with_context(|| format!("expected unsigned integer value, got {raw:?}")),
+        Some(serde_json::Value::Number(_)) => raw
+            .parse::<f64>()
+            .ok()
+            .and_then(serde_json::Number::from_f64)
+            .map(serde_json::Value::Number)
+            .with_context(|| format!("expected floating-point value, got {raw:?}")),
+        Some(serde_json::Value::Array(_)) | Some(serde_json::Value::Object(_)) => {
+            serde_json::from_str(raw).context("expected JSON array/object value")
+        }
+        Some(serde_json::Value::Null) => {
+            if raw.is_empty() || raw.eq_ignore_ascii_case("null") {
+                Ok(serde_json::Value::Null)
+            } else if let Ok(v) = serde_json::from_str(raw) {
+                Ok(v)
+            } else {
+                Ok(serde_json::Value::String(raw.to_string()))
+            }
+        }
+        Some(serde_json::Value::String(_)) | None => Ok(serde_json::Value::String(raw.to_string())),
+    }
+}
+
+fn json_set_path(
+    value: &mut serde_json::Value,
+    parts: &[&str],
+    new_value: serde_json::Value,
+) -> Result<()> {
+    if parts.is_empty() {
+        bail!("property path cannot be empty");
+    }
+    let mut current = value;
+    for part in &parts[..parts.len() - 1] {
+        current = current
+            .as_object_mut()
+            .and_then(|map| map.get_mut(*part))
+            .with_context(|| format!("unknown property section: {part}"))?;
+    }
+    let key = parts[parts.len() - 1];
+    let map = current
+        .as_object_mut()
+        .with_context(|| format!("property parent is not an object: {}", parts[..parts.len() - 1].join(".")))?;
+    if !map.contains_key(key) {
+        bail!("unknown property: {}", parts.join("."));
+    }
+    map.insert(key.to_string(), new_value);
+    Ok(())
+}
+
+fn default_section_value(section: &str) -> Option<serde_json::Value> {
+    let default = serde_json::to_value(Config::default()).ok()?;
+    json_get_path(&default, &split_prop_path(section).ok()?).cloned()
+}
+
+

     format!(
         "Failed to create config directory: {}. If running as an OpenRC service, \

                         .map(|t| t.keys().cloned().collect())

+                    // accepted top-level config keys and are addressable via

+                        "api_url",

+impl Configurable for Config {
+    fn prop_fields(&self) -> Vec<PropField> {
+        let Ok(value) = serde_json::to_value(self) else {
+            return Vec::new();
+        };
+        let mut fields = Vec::new();
+        walk_prop_fields("", &value, &mut fields);
+        fields.sort_by(|a, b| a.path.cmp(&b.path));
+        fields
+    }
+
+    fn get_prop(&self, path: &str) -> Result<Option<serde_json::Value>> {
+        let parts = split_prop_path(path)?;
+        let value = serde_json::to_value(self).context("failed to serialize config")?;
+        if is_virtual_channel_enabled_path(&parts) {
+            let enabled = json_get_path(&value, &parts[..2])
+                .is_some_and(|channel| !channel.is_null());
+            return Ok(Some(serde_json::Value::Bool(enabled)));
+        }
+        Ok(json_get_path(&value, &parts).cloned())
+    }
+
+    fn set_prop(&mut self, path: &str, raw_value: &str) -> Result<()> {
+        let parts = split_prop_path(path)?;
+        if matches!(parts.first(), Some(&"workspace_dir" | &"config_path")) {
+            bail!("{path} is computed at runtime and cannot be set");
+        }
+
+        if is_virtual_channel_enabled_path(&parts) {
+            let enabled = raw_value
+                .parse::<bool>()
+                .with_context(|| format!("expected boolean value, got {raw_value:?}"))?;
+            let mut value = serde_json::to_value(&*self).context("failed to serialize config")?;
+            let section_path = parts[..2].join(".");
+            let new_value = if enabled {
+                match json_get_path(&value, &parts[..2]).cloned() {
+                    Some(current) if !current.is_null() => current,
+                    _ => {
+                        let default_value = default_section_value(&section_path).with_context(|| {
+                            format!("no default is available for section {section_path}")
+                        })?;
+                        if default_value.is_null() {
+                            bail!(
+                                "{section_path} has required fields; initialize it by setting those fields first"
+                            );
+                        }
+                        default_value
+                    }
+                }
+            } else {
+                serde_json::Value::Null
+            };
+            json_set_path(&mut value, &parts[..2], new_value)?;
+            let mut updated: Config =
+                serde_json::from_value(value).context("failed to update config")?;
+            updated.workspace_dir = self.workspace_dir.clone();
+            updated.config_path = self.config_path.clone();
+            *self = updated;
+            return Ok(());
+        }
+
+
+        let mut value = serde_json::to_value(&*self).context("failed to serialize config")?;
+
+        let existing = json_get_path(&value, &parts).cloned();
+        if existing.is_none() {
+            bail!("unknown property: {path}");
+        }
+        let new_value = coerce_prop_value(raw_value, existing.as_ref())?;
+        json_set_path(&mut value, &parts, new_value)?;
+
+        let mut updated: Config = serde_json::from_value(value).context("failed to update config")?;
+        updated.workspace_dir = self.workspace_dir.clone();
+        updated.config_path = self.config_path.clone();
+        *self = updated;
+        Ok(())
+    }
+
+    fn prop_is_secret(path: &str) -> bool {
+        props_is_secret_path(path)
+    }
+
+    fn init_defaults(&mut self, section: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<String>> {
+        let mut initialized = Vec::new();
+        let mut value = serde_json::to_value(&*self).context("failed to serialize config")?;
+
+        if let Some(section) = section {
+            let parts = split_prop_path(section)?;
+            let current = json_get_path(&value, &parts)
+                .with_context(|| format!("unknown config section: {section}"))?;
+            if !current.is_null() {
+                return Ok(initialized);
+            }
+            let default_value = default_section_value(section)
+                .with_context(|| format!("no default is available for section {section}"))?;
+            json_set_path(&mut value, &parts, default_value)?;
+            initialized.push(section.to_string());
+        } else {
+            let default = serde_json::to_value(Config::default()).context("failed to serialize defaults")?;
+            if let (Some(channels), Some(default_channels)) = (
+                value
+                    .get_mut("channels_config")
+                    .and_then(serde_json::Value::as_object_mut),
+                default
+                    .get("channels_config")
+                    .and_then(serde_json::Value::as_object),
+            ) {
+                for (name, current) in channels.iter_mut() {
+                    if current.is_null() {
+                        if let Some(default_value) = default_channels.get(name) {
+                            if !default_value.is_null() {
+                                *current = default_value.clone();
+                                initialized.push(format!("channels_config.{name}"));
+                            }
+                        }
+                    }
+                }
+            }
+        }
+
+        if !initialized.is_empty() {
+            let mut updated: Config = serde_json::from_value(value).context("failed to initialize config defaults")?;
+            updated.workspace_dir = self.workspace_dir.clone();
+            updated.config_path = self.config_path.clone();
+            *self = updated;
+        }
+        Ok(initialized)
+    }
+}
+

 async fn sync_directory(path: &Path) -> Result<()> {
     #[cfg(unix)]

@@ -12,3 +12,57 @@ pub trait ConfigHandle {
     fn name(&self) -> &'static str;
     fn desc(&self) -> &'static str;
 }
+
+/// User-facing primitive kind for a configurable property.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
+pub enum PropKind {
+    String,
+    Integer,
+    Float,
+    Boolean,
+    Array,
+    Object,
+    Null,
+}
+
+impl std::fmt::Display for PropKind {
+    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
+        let s = match self {
+            Self::String => "string",
+            Self::Integer => "integer",
+            Self::Float => "float",
+            Self::Boolean => "bool",
+            Self::Array => "array",
+            Self::Object => "object",
+            Self::Null => "optional",
+        };
+        f.write_str(s)
+    }
+}
+
+/// Metadata for a single property path in `config.toml`.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
+pub struct PropField {
+    pub path: String,
+    pub kind: PropKind,
+    pub secret: bool,
+    pub category: String,
+}
+
+/// Runtime configurable-property access.
+pub trait Configurable {
+    fn prop_fields(&self) -> Vec<PropField>;
+    fn get_prop(&self, path: &str) -> anyhow::Result<Option<serde_json::Value>>;
+    fn set_prop(&mut self, path: &str, value: &str) -> anyhow::Result<()>;
+    fn prop_is_secret(path: &str) -> bool;
+    fn init_defaults(&mut self, section: Option<&str>) -> anyhow::Result<Vec<String>>;
+    fn secret_fields(&self) -> Vec<PropField> {
+        self.prop_fields().into_iter().filter(|f| f.secret).collect()
+    }
+    fn encrypt_secrets(&mut self) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
+        Ok(())
+    }
+    fn decrypt_secrets(&mut self) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
+        Ok(())
+    }
+}

index a52b325..22151ec 100644
--- a/src/main.rs
+++ b/src/main.rs
@@ -38,6 +38,8 @@ use clap::{CommandFactory, Parser, Subcommand, ValueEnum};
 use dialoguer::Password;
 use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
 use std::io::{IsTerminal, Write};
+use crate::config::traits::Configurable;
+
 use std::path::PathBuf;

+#[derive(Subcommand, Debug)]
+enum PropsCommands {
+    /// List configurable properties
+    List {
+        /// Only show paths beginning with this prefix
+        #[arg(long)]
+        filter: Option<String>,
+        /// Include secret properties in output
+        #[arg(long)]
+        secrets: bool,
+    },
+    /// Get one property value
+    Get {
+        /// Dot-separated property path
+        path: String,
+    },
+    /// Set one property value
+    Set {
+        /// Dot-separated property path
+        path: String,
+        /// New value. Omit for a hidden prompt when setting secrets.
+        value: Option<String>,
+    },
+    /// Initialize a missing optional section from defaults
+    Init {
+        /// Optional section path to initialize
+        section: Option<String>,
+    },
+    /// Complete property paths for dynamic shell completion
+    Complete {
+        /// Partial property path
+        partial: Option<String>,
+    },
+}

+    // Dynamic property completion is used by shell completion hooks and must
+    // keep stdout free of logs.  Load config before installing a tracing
+    // subscriber so any diagnostics are suppressed for this narrow path.
+    if let Commands::Props {
+        props_command: PropsCommands::Complete { partial },

+async fn handle_props_command(props_command: PropsCommands, config: &mut Config) -> Result<()> {
+    match props_command {
+        PropsCommands::List { filter, secrets } => {
+            for field in config.prop_fields() {
+                if !secrets && field.secret {
+                    continue;
+                }
+                if let Some(prefix) = filter.as_deref() {
+                    if !field.path.starts_with(prefix) {
+                        continue;
+                    }
+                }
+                let value = if field.secret {
+                    match config.get_prop(&field.path)? {
+                        Some(serde_json::Value::Null) | None => "<unset>".to_string(),
+                        Some(serde_json::Value::String(s)) if s.is_empty() => "<unset>".to_string(),
+                        Some(_) => "<set>".to_string(),
+                    }
+                } else {
+                    config
+                        .get_prop(&field.path)?
+                        .map(|v| prop_value_to_display(&v))
+                        .unwrap_or_else(|| "<unset>".to_string())
+                };
+                println!("{}\t{}\t{}\t{}", field.path, field.kind, field.category, value);
+            }
+            Ok(())
+        }
+        PropsCommands::Get { path } => {
+            let value = config
+                .get_prop(&path)?
+                .with_context(|| format!("unknown property: {path}"))?;
+            if Config::prop_is_secret(&path) {
+                let status = match value {
+                    serde_json::Value::Null => "unset",
+                    serde_json::Value::String(ref s) if s.is_empty() => "unset",
+                    _ => "set",
+                };
+                println!("{status}");
+            } else {
+                println!("{}", prop_value_to_display(&value));
+            }
+            Ok(())
+        }
+        PropsCommands::Set { path, value } => {
+            let value = match value {
+                Some(v) => v,
+                None if Config::prop_is_secret(&path) => Password::new()
+                    .with_prompt(format!("Value for {path}"))
+                    .interact()
+                    .context("failed to read secret value")?,
+                None => bail!("missing value for non-secret property {path}"),
+            };
+            config.set_prop(&path, &value)?;
+            config.save().await?;
+            println!("Updated {path}");
+            Ok(())
+        }
+        PropsCommands::Init { section } => {
+            let initialized = config.init_defaults(section.as_deref())?;
+            if initialized.is_empty() {
+                println!("Nothing to initialize");
+            } else {
+                config.save().await?;
+                for path in initialized {
+                    println!("Initialized {path}");
+                }
+            }
+            Ok(())
+        }
+        PropsCommands::Complete { partial } => {
+            let partial = partial.unwrap_or_default();
+            for field in config.prop_fields() {
+                if field.path.starts_with(&partial) {
+                    println!("{}", field.path);
+                }
+            }
+            Ok(())
+        }
+    }
+}
+
+fn prop_value_to_display(value: &serde_json::Value) -> String {
+    match value {
+        serde_json::Value::String(s) => s.clone(),
+        serde_json::Value::Null => "<unset>".to_string(),
+        _ => serde_json::to_string(value).unwrap_or_else(|_| value.to_string()),
+    }
+}
+
 fn handle_estop_command(
     config: &Config,
     estop_command: Option<EstopSubcommands>,
